note: This zoning system is created to group nearby areas together and use a center point with a radius so that delivery or service requests are never missed.

zones.description: This is the main and most populated area of Islamabad. It includes all F sectors and central G sectors. Most commercial and residential requests come from here.

centerPoint.explanation: This point is roughly in the middle of F and G sectors, so a radius from here covers all nearby sectors.

zones.radiusExplanation: A 10 km radius safely covers all F and G sectors even if the user's location is slightly outside sector boundaries.
zones.example: If a user selects G-10 or F-8, this zone should be used.

zones.zoneName: North & West Islamabad (D, E, C, B Sectors)
zones.description: This zone includes developing and less dense sectors of Islamabad, mostly towards the north and west.

centerPoint.explanation: This point lies near D-12 and E-11, which helps cover northern and western sectors evenly.

zones.radiusExplanation: 10 km radius ensures no request is missed even if housing societies are slightly spread out.
zones.example: If a user is in D-12 or B-17, this zone should be selected.

zones.description: This zone covers southern and eastern Islamabad including industrial and mixed residential areas.

centerPoint.explanation: This location is near I-10, making it a good center for southern Islamabad.

zones.radiusExplanation: 9 km radius is enough to cover I and H sectors without overlapping too much into Rawalpindi.
zones.example: If a user requests service from I-10 or H-8, use this zone.

zones.description: Rawalpindi does not have sectors like Islamabad, so major neighborhoods are grouped together.

centerPoint.explanation: This is near Saddar Rawalpindi, a central and well-known location.

zones.radiusExplanation: 12 km radius is required because Rawalpindi areas are spread out and not grid-based.
zones.example: If a user selects Satellite Town or Saddar, this zone applies.

zones.description: This zone is for areas slightly outside main Islamabad and Rawalpindi but still serviceable.

centerPoint.explanation: This center helps balance coverage between Islamabad and Rawalpindi outskirts.

zones.radiusExplanation: A larger radius is used here because these areas are far apart and less structured.
zones.example: If a request comes from DHA or Bahria Enclave, use this zone.

zones.description: This is the main commercial and upscale residential area of Karachi.

centerPoint.explanation: Central point for South Karachi covering DHA and Saddar.

zones.radiusExplanation: A 12 km radius safely covers South Karachi including Clifton and DHA.
zones.example: If a user selects Clifton or DHA, this zone should be used.

zones.description: This zone includes major residential and commercial hubs in East Karachi.

centerPoint.explanation: Lies near Gulshan and Jauhar to cover the eastern region.

zones.radiusExplanation: 15 km radius covers these dense residential areas efficiently.
zones.example: If a user is in Gulshan-e-Iqbal or PECHS, this zone applies.

zones.description: This zone covers central portions of the city including busy middle-class neighborhoods.

centerPoint.explanation: Central point for Nazimabad and Federal B Area.

zones.radiusExplanation: 12 km radius covers Central Karachi neighborhoods adequately.
zones.example: If a request comes from Nazimabad or FB Area, use this zone.

zones.description: This zone covers the eastern outskirts and industrial sectors.

centerPoint.explanation: Centered around Malir and Korangi industrial zones.

zones.radiusExplanation: Large radius due to the spread-out nature of Malir and Korangi.
zones.example: If a user selects Malir or Korangi, this zone should be used.

zones.description: Covers western industrial areas and port zones.

centerPoint.explanation: Central for West Karachi covering SITE and Orangi.

zones.radiusExplanation: 15 km covers the sprawling western zones.
zones.example: If a user is in Orangi or Keamari, this zone is applied.

importantNotesForBeginners: Always check the user's latitude and longitude against the zone center and radius.
If a user's location falls within the radius, consider it covered by that zone.
Radius is intentionally kept slightly larger to avoid missing edge cases.
Islamabad uses sectors, Rawalpindi uses area names — do not confuse them.
If a location falls in two zones, choose the nearer zone center.
